verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Follow peace with all men, and holiness, without which no one shall see the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: There are many things characteristic of Christianity: but more than all, and better than all, Love towards one another, and Peace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore Christ also saith, “My peace I give unto you.” ( John xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, “By this shall all men know that ye are My disciples, if ye love one another.” ( John xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore Paul too says, “Follow peace with all men, and holiness,” that is, purity, “without which no man shall see the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Looking diligently lest any man fail of the grace of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: As if they were traveling together on some long journey, in a large company, he says, Take heed that no man be left behind: I do not seek this only, that ye should arrive yourselves, but also that ye should look diligently after the others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Lest any man” (he says) “fail of the grace of God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: (He means the good things to come, the faith of the gospel, the best course of life: for they all are of “the Grace of God.”)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Do not tell me, It is [but] one that perisheth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Even for one Christ died.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Hast thou no care for him “for whom Christ died”? ( 1 Cor. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Looking diligently,” he saith, that is, searching carefully, considering, thoroughly ascertaining, as is done in the case of sick persons, and in all ways examining, thoroughly ascertaining.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you.” ( Deut. xxix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This is found in Deuteronomy; and he derived it from the metaphor of plants.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Lest any root of bitterness,” he says; which he said also in another place when he writes, “A little leaven leaveneth the whole lump.” ( 1 Cor. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Not for his sake alone do I wish this, he means, but also on account of the harm arising therefrom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That is to say, even if there be a root of this kind, do not suffer any shoot to come up, but let it be cut off, that it may not bear its proper fruits, that so it may not defile and pollute the others also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For, he saith, “Lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you; and by it many be defiled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And with good reason did he call sin “bitter”: for truly nothing is more bitter than sin, and they know it, who after they have committed it pine away under their conscience, who endure much bitterness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For being exceedingly bitter, it perverts the reasoning faculty itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Such is the nature of what is bitter: it is unprofitable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And well said he, “root of bitterness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He said not, “bitter,” but “of bitterness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it is possible that a bitter root might bear sweet fruits; but it is not possible that a root and fountain and foundation of bitterness, should ever bear sweet fruit; for all is bitter, it has nothing sweet, all are bitter, all unpleasant, all full of hatred and abomination.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “And by this” (he says) “many be defiled.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That is, Cut off the lascivious persons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Lest there be any fornicator: or profane person, as Esau, who for one morsel of meat sold his birthright.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And wherein was Esau a “fornicator”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He does not say that Esau was a fornicator.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Lest there be any fornicator,” he says, then, “follow after holiness: lest there be any, as Esau, profane”: that is, gluttonous, without self-control, worldly, selling away things spiritual.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Who for one morsel of meat sold his birthright,” who through his own slothfulness sold this honor which he had from God, and for a little pleasure, lost the greatest honor and glory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This was suitable to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: This [was the conduct] of an abominable, of an unclean person.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So that not only is the fornicator unclean, but also the glutton, the slave of his belly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he also is a slave of a different pleasure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He is forced to be overreaching, he is forced to be rapacious, to behave himself unseemly in ten thousand ways, being the slave of that passion, and oftentimes he blasphemes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So he accounted “his birthright” to be nothing worth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: That is, providing for temporary refreshment, he went even to the [sacrifice of his] “birthright.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So henceforth “the birthright” belongs to us, not to the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And at the same time also this is added to their calamity, that the first is become last, and the second, first: the one, for courageous endurance; the other last for indolence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For ye know” (he says) “how that afterward, when he would have inherited the blessing, he was rejected.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he found no place of repentance, though he sought it carefully with tears.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What now is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Doth he indeed exclude repentance?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: ‘But how, you say, was it that “he found no place of repentance”?’

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if he condemned himself, if he made a great wailing, why did he “find no place of repentance”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Because it was not really a case of repentance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For as the grief of Cain was not of repentance, and the murder proved it; so also in this case, his words were not those of repentance, and the murder afterwards proved it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For even he also in intention slew Jacob.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For “The days of mourning for my father,” he said, “are at hand; then will I slay my brother Jacob.” ( Gen. xxvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Tears” had not power to give him “repentance.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And [the Apostle] did not say “by repentance” simply, but even “with tears, he found no place of repentance.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Because he did not repent as he ought, for this is repentance; he repented not as it behoved him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For how is it that he [the Apostle] said this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How did he exhort them again after they had become “sluggish” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How, when they were become “lame”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How, when they were “paralyzed” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How, when they were “relaxed” ( ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For this is the beginning of a fall.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He seems to me to hint at some fornicators amongst them, but not to wish at that time to correct them: but feigns ignorance that they might correct themselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it is right at first indeed to pretend ignorance: but afterwards, when they continue [in sin], then to add reproof also, that so they may not become shameless.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Which Moses also did in the case of Zimri and the daughter of Cosbi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “For he found” (he says) “no place of repentance,” he found not repentance; or that he sinned beyond repentance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: There are then sins beyond repentance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: His meaning is, Let us not fall by an incurable fall.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So long as it is a matter of lameness, it is easy to become upright: but if we turn out of the way, what will be left?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it is to those who have not yet fallen that he thus discourses, striking them with terror, and says that it is not possible for him who is fallen to obtain consolation; but to those who have fallen, that they may not fall into despair, he says the contrary, speaking thus, “My little children, of whom I travail in birth again, until Christ he formed in you.” ( Gal. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, “Whosoever of you are justified by the Law, are fallen from Grace.” ( Gal. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Lo! he testifies that they had fallen away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he that standeth, hearing that it is not possible to obtain pardon after having fallen, will be more zealous, and more cautious about his standing: if however thou use the same violence towards one also who is fallen, he will never rise again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For by what hope will he show forth the change?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But he not only wept (you say), but also “sought earnestly.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He does not then exclude repentance; but makes them careful not to fall.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: As many then as do not believe in Hell, let them call these things to mind: as many as think to sin without being punished, let them take account of these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Why did Esau not obtain pardon?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Because he repented not as he ought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Wouldest thou see perfect repentance?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Hear of the repentance of Peter after his denial.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For the Evangelist in relating to us the things concerning him, says, “And he went out and wept bitterly.” ( Matt. xxvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore even such a sin was forgiven him, because he repented as he ought.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Although the Victim had not yet been offered, nor had The Sacrifice as yet been made, nor was sin as yet-taken away, it still had the rule and sovereignty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And that thou mayest learn, that this denial [arose] not so much from sloth, as from His being forsaken of God, who was teaching him to know the measures of man and not to contradict the sayings of the Master, nor to be more high-minded than the rest, but to know that nothing can be done without God, and that “Except the Lord build the house, they labor in vain who build it” ( Ps. cxxvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 1 ): therefore also Christ said to him alone, “Satan desired to sift thee as wheat,” and I allowed it not, “that thy faith may not fail.” ( Luke xxii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For since it was likely that he would be high-minded, being conscious to himself that he loved Christ more than they all, therefore “he wept bitterly”; and he did other things after his weeping, of the same character.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For what did he do?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: After this he exposed himself to dangers innumerable, and by many means showed his manliness and courage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Judas also repented, but in an evil way: for he hanged himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Esau too repented; as I said; or rather, he did not even repent; for his tears were not [tears] of repentance, but rather of pride and wrath.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And what followed proved this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: The blessed David repented, thus saying, “Every night will I wash my bed: I will water my couch with my tears.” ( Ps. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And the sin which had been committed long ago, after so many years, after so many generations he bewailed, as if it had recently occurred.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For he who repents ought not to be angry, nor to be fierce, but to be contrite, as one condemned, as not having boldness, as one on whom sentence has been passed, as one who ought to be saved by mercy alone, as one who has shown himself ungrateful toward his Benefactor, as unthankful, as reprobate, as worthy of punishments innumerable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If he considers these things, he will not be angry, he will not be indignant, but will mourn, will weep, will groan, and lament night and day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: He that is penitent ought never to forget his sin, but on the one hand, to beseech God not to remember it; while on the other, he himself never forgets it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If we remember it, God will forget it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us exact punishment from ourselves; let us accuse ourselves; thus shall we propitiate the Judge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For sin confessed becomes less, but not confessed worse.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For if sin add to itself shamelessness and ingratitude, how will he who does not know that he sinned before be at all able to guard himself from falling again into the same [evils]?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us then not deny [our sins], I beseech you, nor be shameless, that we may not unwillingly pay the penalty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Cain heard God say, “Where is Abel thy brother?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And he said, I know not; am I my brother’s keeper?” ( Gen. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou how this made his sin more grievous?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But his father did not act thus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: When he heard, “Adam, where art thou?” ( Gen. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 9 ), he said, “I heard Thy voice, and I was afraid, because I am naked, and I hid myself.” ( Gen. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It is a great good to acknowledge our sins, and to bear them in mind continually.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Nothing so effectually cures a fault, as a continual remembrance of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Nothing makes a man so slow to wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I know that conscience starts back, and endures not to be scourged by the remembrance of evil deeds; but hold tight thy soul and place a muzzle on it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For like an ill-broken horse, so it bears impatiently [what is put upon it], and is unwilling to persuade itself that it has sinned: but all this is the work of Satan.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But let us persuade it that it has sinned; let us persuade it that it has sinned, that it may also repent, in order that having repented it may escape torment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: How dost thou think to obtain pardon for thy sins, tell me, when thou hast not yet confessed them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Assuredly he is worthy of compassion and kindness who has sinned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But thou who hast not yet persuaded thyself [that thou hast sinned], how dost thou think to be pitied, when thou art thus without shame for some things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us persuade ourselves that we have sinned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us say it not with the tongue only, but also with the mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us not call ourselves sinners, but also count over our sins, going over them each specifically.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I do not say to thee, Make a parade of thyself, nor accuse thyself before others: but be persuaded by the prophet when he saith, “Reveal thy way unto the Lord.” ( Ps. xxxvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Confess these things before God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Confess before the Judge thy sins with prayer; if not with tongue, yet in memory, and be worthy of mercy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: If thou keep thy sins continually in remembrance, thou wilt never bear in mind the wrongs of thy neighbor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I do not say, if thou art persuaded that thou art thyself a sinner; this does not avail so to humble the soul, as sins themselves [taken] by themselves, and examined specifically.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou wilt have no remembrance of wrongs [done thee], if thou hast these things continually in remembrance; thou wilt feel no anger, thou wilt not revile, thou wilt have no high thoughts, thou wilt not fall again into the same [sins], thou wilt be more earnest towards good things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Seest thou how many excellent [effects] are produced from the remembrance of our sins?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us then write them in our minds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: I know that the soul does not endure a recollection which is so bitter: but let us constrain and force it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: It is better that it should be gnawed with the remembrance now, than at that time with vengeance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Now, if thou remember them, and continually present them before God (see p.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 448), and pray for them, thou wilt speedily blot them out; but if thou forget them now, thou wilt then be reminded of them even against thy will, when they are brought out publicly before the whole world, displayed before all, both friends and enemies, and Angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For surely He did not say to David only, “What thou didst secretly, I will make manifest to” ( 2 Sam. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 12 ) all, but even to us all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Thou wert afraid of men (he said) and respected them more than God; and God seeing thee, thou caredst not, but wert ashamed before men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it says, “the eyes of men, this is their fear.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Therefore thou shalt suffer punishment in that very point; for I will reprove thee, setting thy sins before the eyes of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For that this is true, and that in that day the sins of us all are [to be] publicly displayed, unless we now do them away by continual remembrance, hear how cruelty and inhumanity are publicly exposed, “I was an hungered” (He says) “and ye gave Me no meat.” ( Matt. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: When are these things said?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Is it in a corner?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Is it in a secret place?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “When the Son of Man shall come in His glory” ( Matt. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 31, 32 ), and “all the nations” are gathered together, when He has separated the one from the other, then will He speak in the audience of all, and will “set” them “on His right hand” and “on” His “left” ( Matt. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 33 ): “I was an hungered and ye gave Me no meat.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: See again the five virgins also, hearing before all, “I know you not.” ( Matt. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For the five and five do not set forth the number of five only, but those virgins who are wicked and cruel and inhuman, and those who are not such.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: So also he that buried his one talent, heard before all, even of those who had brought the five and the two, “Thou wicked and slothful servant.” ( Matt. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But not by words alone, but by deeds also does He then convict them: even as the Evangelist also says, “They shall look on Him whom they pierced.” ( John xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For the resurrection shall be of all at the same time, of sinners and of the righteous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: At the same time shall He be present to all in the judgment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Consider therefore who they are who shall then be in dismay, who in grief, who dragged away to the fire, while the others are crowned.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: “Come” (He says), “ye blessed of My Father, inherit the kingdom which hath been prepared for you from the foundation of the world.” ( Matt. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And again, “Depart from Me into the fire which hath been prepared for the devil and his angels.” ( Matt. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Let us not merely hear the words but write them also before our sight, and let us imagine Him to be now present and saying these things, and that we are led away to that fire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What heart shall we have?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What consolation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And what, when we are cut asunder?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: And what when we are accused of rapacity?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What excuse shall we have to utter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What specious argument?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: None: but of necessity bound, bending down, we must be dragged to the mouths of the furnace, to the river of fire, to the darkness, to then ever-dying punishments, and entreat no one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For it is not, it is not possible, He says, to pass across from this side to that: for “there is a great gulf betwixt us and you” ( Luke xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 26 ), and it is not possible even for those who wish it to go across, and stretch out a helping hand: but we must needs burn continually, no one aiding us, even should it be father or mother, or any whosoever, yea though he have much boldness toward God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For, it says, “A brother doth not redeem; shall man redeem?” ( Ps. xlix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: Since then it is not possible to have one’s hopes of salvation in another, but [it must be] in one’s self after the lovingkindness of God, let us do all things, I entreat you, so that our conduct may be pure, and our course of life the best, and that it may not receive any stain even from the beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But if not, at all events, let us not sleep after the stain, but continue always washing away the pollution by repentance, by tears, by prayers, by works of mercy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: What then, you say, if I cannot do works of mercy?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But thou hast “a cup of cold water” ( Matt. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 42 ), however poor thou art.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: But thou hast “two mites” ( Mark xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: 42 ), in whatever poverty thou art; but thou hast feet, so as to visit the sick, so as to enter into a prison; but thou hast a roof, so as to receive strangers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For there is no pardon, no, none for him who does not do works of mercy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: These things we say to you continually, that we may effect if it be but a little by the continued repetition: these things we say, not caring so much for those who receive the benefits, as for yourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Epistle to the Hebrews.
verses.text: For ye give to them indeed things here, but in return you receive heavenly things: which may we all obtain, in Christ Jesus our Lord, with whom to the Father be glory, together with the Holy Ghost, now and ever, and world without end.
